sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id Int64, value1 Int64, value2 Int64 DEFAULT NULL);
SELECT id, bitHammingDistance(value1 * 2, value2 * 3) AS distance FROM test_table WHERE value1 IS NOT NULL AND value2 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, value1, value2, bitHammingDistance(value1, value2) * 2 AS scaled_distance FROM test_table HAVING scaled_distance > 2;
SELECT id, (SELECT COUNT() FROM test_table AS inner_tbl WHERE inner_tbl.value1 * 2 = outer_tbl.value1) AS inner_count FROM test_table AS outer_tbl;
SELECT COUNT() AS total_count, bitHammingDistance((value1 * 2) + 1, value2) AS distance FROM test_table GROUP BY distance ORDER BY total_count DESC;
CREATE TABLE temp_table (id Int64, bit_val1 Int64, bit_val2 Int64);
SELECT bitHammingDistance(bit_val1, bit_val2) AS distance FROM temp_table WHERE bit_val2 IS NOT NULL OR bit_val1 > 5;
SELECT bitHammingDistance((SELECT MIN(value1) FROM test_table), (SELECT MAX(value2) FROM test_table)) AS extreme_distance;

SELECT outer_tbl.id, (SELECT bitHammingDistance(inner_tbl.value1, inner_tbl.value2) FROM test_table AS inner_tbl WHERE inner_tbl.id = outer_tbl.id) AS inner_distance FROM test_table AS outer_tbl WHERE outer_tbl.value1 IS NOT NULL;
ALTER TABLE test_table ADD COLUMN new_value Int64 DEFAULT 0;
UPDATE test_table SET new_value = bitHammingDistance(value1, value2) WHERE value1 IS NOT NULL AND value2 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, bitHammingDistance(value1 * new_value + 1, value2 * new_value) AS combined_distance FROM test_table;
